channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: 2027: Tinubu presents Shettima APC VP nomination certificate
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
President Tinubu officially presents VP Kashim Shettima with his APC vice-presidential nomination certificate for the 2027 general election.
Read More: https://punchng.com/2027-tinubu-presents-shettima-apc-vp-nomination-certificate/

item.title: 100,000 firms risk delisting as CAC enforces compliance
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The Corporate Affairs Commission (CAC) is delisting 100,000 firms for non-compliance. Learn about the 90-day window to avoid deregistration.
Read More: https://punchng.com/100000-firms-risk-delisting-as-cac-enforces-compliance/

item.title: Messi falls short of Okocha’s enduring record
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Lionel Messi’s masterclass against England wasn’t enough to break Jay-Jay Okocha’s 1994 World Cup dribbling record. Read more on this enduring football fea
Read More: https://punchng.com/messi-falls-short-of-okochas-enduring-record/

item.title: Lagos blames heavy rainfall for worsening road conditions
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Lagos State Public Works Corporation attributes worsening Lagos roads to heavy rainfall, assuring residents of ongoing efforts to repair damaged infrastruc
Read More: https://punchng.com/lagos-blames-heavy-rainfall-for-worsening-road-conditions/

item.title: Okoye rules out Udinese exit
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Super Eagles goalkeeper Maduka Okoye confirms he will not be leaving Udinese this summer, despite interest from Juventus and other top European clubs.
Read More: https://punchng.com/okoye-rules-out-udinese-exit/

item.title: Two nabbed as police recover stolen N7.5m Adamawa vehicle
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Adamawa police recover a N7.5m stolen Toyota Hilux and arrest two suspects involved in the criminal conspiracy and receiving of the stolen Adamawa vehicle.
Read More: https://punchng.com/two-nabbed-as-police-recover-stolen-n7-5m-adamawa-vehicle/

item.title: Offiong Edem tourney launched my Table Tennis journey – Udoh
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Rising table tennis player Victor Udoh has credited the Offiong Edem Championship with providing the platform that helped shape his competitive career, describing the tournament as a major turning point in his development. Speaking in an interview, Udoh recalled that his journey into the sport began

item.title: Young Nigerians lift African Flag Football crown
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Nigeria’s Under-13 flag football team has emerged champions of the 2026 NFL FLAG Africa Continental Championship in Nairobi, Kenya, defeating some of Africa’s strongest emerging teams to lift the continental trophy and further underline the country’s growing influence in the sport. The post Young Ni

item.title: Micron has turned into ‘the most important stock in the market.’ So is it time to worry?
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: While investors are concerned about a memory peak, analysts say the stock still looks cheap relative to various earnings scenarios.

item.title: Police confirm 2 deaths in Osun violence, Adeleke reacts
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Osun State Police Command has confirmed that two persons were killed in separate violent clashes in the Egbatedo and Owode-Igbona areas of Osogbo. The post Police confirm 2 deaths in Osun violence, Adeleke reacts appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Armed herdsmen attack another Benue community, shoot resident, loot shops
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Armed men suspected to be herdsmen have again attacked Ankpechi community in Oglewu Ward of Ohimini Local Government Area of Benue State, shooting one resident and forcing villagers to flee their homes. The post Armed herdsmen attack another Benue community, shoot resident, loot shops appeared first

item.title: Habila’s death: Umahi requests CP to commence forensic autopsy
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: As controversy continues to trail the death of Miss Mary Habila, the Minister of Works, Senator David Umahi, has formally requested the Commissioner of Police in Ebonyi State to commence a comprehensive forensic autopsy on the remains of Habila, who was one of his staff members. The post Habila’s de

item.title: 2027 elections: Police set to mop up illegal arms nationwide to curb violence
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Ahead of the 2027 general elections, The Inspector-General of Police(IGP), Olatunji Disu said the Nigeria Police Force had commenced a programme to mop up illegal arms across the country. The post 2027 elections: Police set to mop up illegal arms nationwide to curb violence appeared first on The Sun

item.title: Okpebholo lauded over training of 500 Edo forest guards
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Office of the National Security Adviser (ONSA) has commended Edo State Governor, Senator Monday Okpebholo, for the ongoing training of 500 forest guards aimed at strengthening security across the state. The post Okpebholo lauded over training of 500 Edo forest guards appeared first on The Sun Ni

item.title: Inter-agency training our priority for Lagos security architecture – Ogunsan
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Lagos State Security Trust Fund (LSSTF) has pledged to make its inter-agency security training a permanent feature of the state’s security architecture as part of efforts to strengthen collaboration, professionalism and operational effectiveness among security agencies. The post Inter-agency tra

item.title: Oriire: Tinubu, security agencies commended over release of abductees
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Human Rights Monitoring Agenda (HURMA) Global Resource Initiative has commended President Bola Ahmed Tinubu and Nigeria’s security agencies for securing the release of kidnapped victims in Oyo State, describing the operation as a demonstration of effective coordination and professionalism. The post

item.title: FTAN charts new pathway for Nigerian tourism
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: •Demands urgent review of tourism laws The Federation of Tourism Associations of Nigeria (FTAN) has called for urgent federal action to resolve what it described as the worst regulatory crisis in the tourism sector, citing confusion and multiple litigation caused by inconsistencies in the NIHOTOUR A

item.title: Lagos NURTW murder: Tijani warns members against breakdown of law
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Commissioner of Police, Lagos State Command, Fatai Tijani, has warned transport union members and other residents against taking the law into their own hands following the gruesome killing of the Organising Secretary of the National Union of Road Transport Workers (NURTW), Toba Ajiboye. The post Lag

item.title: Stakeholders: NCAA Must Seek Additional Funding to Stem Financial Crisis
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Chinedu Eze Industry stakeholders have called on the Nigeria Civil Aviation Authority (NCAA) to seek for additional revenue streams that will boost its finances and avert any form of financial

item.title: Global Food Safety Standards Body Adopts New Guidance on ‘May Contain’
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Allergen Labels Oluchi Chibuzor  The Codex Alimentarius Commission has agreed to adopt new international guidelines on the use of precautionary allergen labelling (PAL), marking an important step towards making “may

item.title: ‘Trump Accounts’ are symbolic, not effective at closing wealth gap
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The wealth gap is wider than it’s been in three decades.

item.title: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un meets with top Chinese official
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The meeting comes after Xi visited Kim, promising closer diplomatic, law enforcement and military cooperation.

item.title: Stock Market Depreciate by 0.09% on Profit-taking in BUA Cement
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Kayode Tokede   The Nigerian stock  market closed lower yesterday  as mid-session profit taking in BUA Cement Plc wiped 0.09 per cent  off investors’ investments. As BUA Cement dropped by 10

item.title: Stakeholders push reforms to sustain Nigeria’s economic gains
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Nigeria’s economic managers and business leaders converge to discuss sustained reforms, private sector investment, and fiscal-monetary coordination to tran
Read More: https://punchng.com/stakeholders-push-reforms-to-sustain-nigerias-economic-gains/

item.title: Private Hajj operator pays N25m commission to 46 marketers, referral partners
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Hajj Mabrur Ventures Limited has disbursed N25 million in commissions, rebates and incentives to 46 accredited marketers, referral partners and stakeholders following the completion of its 2026 Hajj operations. The private Hajj and Umrah operator said the payments were made under its referral and co

item.title: N’East stakeholders hail Tinubu over N80bn flood intervention fund
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
North East stakeholders commend President Tinubu for approving N80bn flood intervention fund, praising its timeliness in mitigating flood impact across Nig
Read More: https://punchng.com/neast-stakeholders-hail-tinubu-over-n80bn-flood-intervention-fund/

A prominent APC chieftain in Gombe State, Aliyu Mohammed, has declared his support for the party’s 2027 governorship candidate, Dr. Jamilu Gwamna, citing G

item.title: NANTA Pushes for Tourism Growth in Africa

item.description: Concerned about the painful impact of xenophobic conversations and trade barriers across the African continent, the National Association of Nigeria Travel Agencies (NANTA) in collaboration with Ghana-based Safari Valley Resort

item.title: How Moniepoint is Powering Nigeria’s $11bn Food Service Industry

item.description: Emma Okonji A new case study by Moniepoint, Africa’s all-in-one financial ecosystem platform for individuals, businesses and their customers, has traced four decades of Nigeria’s food service industry and revealed

item.title: Nigeria’s Renewable Energy Industry Shifts Focus to Storage as Solar Costs Fall
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Kayode Tokede  Falling equipment costs, battery storage and smarter energy infrastructure dominated discussions at Solar & Storage Live Nigeria 2026, as industry leaders said Nigeria’s renewable energy market is entering

item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Kayode Tokede  FATE Foundation, with funding from the Citi Foundation, has launched the Youth Entrepreneurship Incubation Programme to equip young people in Nigeria with financial literacy and entrepreneurship skills.  Delivered

item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Nume Ekeghe Guaranty Trust Bank Limited (GTBank), the flagship banking subsidiary of Guaranty Trust Holding Company Plc (GTCO), has been named the Best Overall Performing Bank in Nigeria in The

item.title: inDrive Expands Driver Support with Healthcare Coverage
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: inDrive, a global mobility and urban services platform, has taken a bold step in advancing driver welfare by providing healthcare coverage for 500 drivers and their families through a partnership

item.title: China Chip Stock Pullback Drives Investor Fear to Four-Year High
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: A measure of investor sentiment on China technology hardware is flashing its most bearish reading in over four years as the red-hot rally in chip stocks rapidly cools.

item.title: Toyosi Olosunde launches ‘Fly Africans’ in UK, profiling Africans making global impact
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Nigerian media personality, broadcaster, interviewer and event host Toyosi Olosunde has officially launched ‘Fly Africans’, a premium interview platform dedicated to documenting the journeys, achievements and impact of exceptional Africans living in the United Kingdom. The platform is designed to fe

item.title: Modi Adviser Urges India to Seek More Chinese Investment, Trade
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: India should deepen economic ties with Beijing by opening the door to greater Chinese investment and reconsidering regional trade pacts as shifting geopolitics make the US a less dependable partner, an adviser to Prime Minister Narendra Modi said.

item.title: Investor Srinivasan Seeks Meeting with Anwar’s Office After Raid
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Investor Balaji Srinivasan requested a meeting with the office of Malaysian Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim, days after immigration officers raided his startup school in Forest City, a mega-development in the southern state of Johor.

item.title: Expand Shari’a court jurisdiction, ex-grand Khadi urges FG
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Former Grand Khadi of the Kwara State Shari’a Court of Appeal, Hon. Justice Solihu Olohuntoyin Muhammad (rtd), has called on the Federal Government to amend the 1999 Constitution to expand the jurisdiction of Shari’a Courts of Appeal beyond Islamic personal law matters, saying the current limitation

item.title: MODERNISING THE ABATTOIRS
item.dc:creator: ThisDay Editorial
item.description: Abattoirs should be operated in a healthy environment The health of citizens should concern every responsible and responsive government, especially when it comes to what they consume daily. Unfortunately, this

item.title: Falling debris from intercepted Iranian strikes sparks fires in Kuwait
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Moment a projectile struck a border area between Kuwait and Iraq, sparking a fire and sending thick smoke into the sky.

item.title: Brazilian football legend Pele’s World Cup shirt sells for $4.9m
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The sale is a record for the Brazilian footballing great, but far from the most expensive piece of sporting memorabilia.

item.title: Taco Bell Pulls Lettuce in Some States After Cyclospora Outbreak
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Taco Bell said it has voluntarily removed potentially affected lettuce from a supplier linked to a multistate Cyclospora outbreak and will replace the ingredient within 24 hours at restaurants in affected states.

item.title: Oloyede calls for renewed vision, hails FOMWAN’s four decades of service
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The outgoing Registrar of the Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board, Prof. Is-haq Oloyede, has commended the Federation of Muslim Women’s Associations in Nigeria (FOMWAN), Oyo State chapter, for its four decades of unwavering commitment to Islamic propagation, education, humanitarian services and

item.title: Kogi announces successful rescue of kidnapped victims in Dekina
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The Kogi State Government has announced the successful rescue of the four persons kidnapped during the recent attack on an unauthorized examination center in Olowa, Dekina Local Government Area of the State. The Commissioner for Information and Communications, Kingsley Femi Fanwo, disclosed this in

item.title: China AI Stocks’ 1000%-Plus Profit Gain May Fail to Revive Rally
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: China’s artificial intelligence companies are poised to deliver robust earnings, but elevated expectations after a months-long rally are raising the bar for tech stocks to climb further.

item.title: Obi urges EFCC to focus on strategic priorities, leave Randy Peter, Mama Pee alone
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Henry Umoru, ABUJA The Presidential Candidate of the Nigeria Democratic Congress (NDC) ahead of the 2027 elections, Peter Obi, has urged the Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC) to focus its attention on major corruption cases and other strategic priorities rather than pursuing social

item.title: Netflix is getting stingier about its viewing data, and Wall Street isn’t happy
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Netflix’s stock is falling in the wake of mixed earnings and a new plan to cut back on the publication of ‘What We Watched’ reports.

item.title: 10 U.S. metro areas where builders are slashing prices on new homes
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Existing-home prices just hit a new record high — but there are plenty of newly built homes selling at a discount, as desperate builders cut prices.

item.title: Lingbot-map: A 3D foundation model for reconstructing scenes from streaming data
item.dc:creator: Hacker News
item.description: Article URL: https://github.com/Robbyant/lingbot-map Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48941908 Points: 5 # Comments: 0

item.title: FG Renames Lagos-Calabar Coastal Highway After Tinubu
item.dc:creator: Information Nigeria
item.description: The Federal Government has announced that the Lagos-Calabar Coastal Highway would henceforth be known as the President Bola Ahmed Tinubu Coastal Highway. According to David Umahi, the minister of works, the federal ministry decided to rename the project in recognition of Tinubu’s role in conce

item.title: Thai Stocks Go From Worst to Best as Funds Eye Further Gains
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Thai stocks are finding favor again as investors bet that an Anutin Charnvirakul-led government will bring an end to years of political turbulence, spurring foreign capital inflows.

item.title: Gold Heads for Weekly Fall as Hormuz Strikes Raise Fed-Hike Bets
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Gold was on track for the biggest weekly loss since early June as renewed hostilities in the Middle East raised the odds that the US Federal Reserve may need to raise interest rates to contain inflation.

item.title: Iran war live: US carries out strikes on civilian infrastructure in south
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Officials report power outages, attack on train station in Bandar, as Iran says Hormuz won't return to pre-war status.

item.title: Blasts reported in Iran as US launches new wave of strikes
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Iran says US strikes hit bridges in the country in an apparent escalation, while the US boards a ship in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Middle East live: US strikes in Iran hit airport, bridges and railway station
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Deadly US strikes overnight hit an airport, a railway station and two bridges in Iran, killing three people, Iranian state media reported on Friday. The United States and Iran have traded fire as the foes battle over the vital Strait of Hormuz waterway in the renewed Middle East war. Follow our liv

item.title: Ogun Muslim Council lauds Fajimite’s emergence as MULAN president
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Ogun State Muslim Council (OMC), under the headship of its President-General, Oba Saka Adelola Matemilola, the Olowu of Owu, has congratulated a son of Ogun State and frontline legal practitioner, Alhaji Hassan Taiwo Fajimite, on his election as the National President of the Muslim Lawyers Assoc

item.title: Venezuela earthquake: Number of known dead rises to nearly 5,000 victims
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The UN estimates 50,000 are missing, with many more feared dead under the rubble as the focus shifts to rebuilding.

item.title: Vietnamese activist Bao Ngoc places rare national spotlight on Gaza
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Activist Bao Ngoc draws attention to plight of Palestinians in country where protest is tightly controlled.

item.title: Palo Alto Networks’ stock has been on a tear — and it could go even higher, according to these bulls
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: The company’s shares have roughly doubled over the past three months, as investors have warmed to the idea that AI is increasing the need for cybersecurity products.

item.title: As SpaceX’s stock notches first close below IPO price, the company is gearing up for a crucial launch
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: The megarocket’s success is SpaceX’s “single most important watch item,” one analyst says.

item.title: Top Currency Forecaster Says Yen May Reach 170 Against Dollar
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: The most-accurate forecaster of dollar-yen in Bloomberg’s latest rankings sees the currency sliding as far as 170 against the dollar next year, using a prediction model that largely ignores the news and focuses on technical analysis instead.

item.title: Celtic struggling to compete in transfer window - CEO
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Celtic are struggling to compete with English Championship clubs in the transfer market and deal with agents, according to minutes of a meeting between board members and supporters.

item.title: Tinubu Launches $3.05bn World Bank-Backed Programmes To Fight Poverty
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: President Bola Tinubu on Thursday launched five World Bank-backed programmes worth $3.05 billion aimed at accelerating poverty reduction, strengthening human capital development, improving livelihoods and building resilient communities across Nigeria. The programmes comprise the Nigeria Community Ac

item.title: PFIPC Scandal: Gbajabiamila Sues Alleged Fake DG For N15bn Over Bribery Claim
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Chief of Staff to the President, Femi Gbajabiamila, has slammed a N15 billion defamation suit on the embattled Director-General of the controversial Presidential Economic Advisory Council/Presidential Foreign Intervention Promotion Council (PFIPC), Adeyemi Adeniyi. Gbajabiamila filed the suit in

item.title: San Francisco mayor pushes for tougher rules after the Waymo traffic fiasco
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: In the wake of a massive hours-long gridlock event, San Francisco mayor Daniel Lurie has told state regulators it's time to put more requirements on robotaxi operators like Waymo.

item.title: NEPL/Seplat Energy JV empowers over 110 youths in Edo
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Held under the theme: “Lighting Up the Nation: Empowering Youths in Renewable Energy Solutions”, the YEP graduation ceremony featured exhibitions of practical solutions developed by participants during the programme, including solar home systems, mini-grid prototypes, solar dryers, biogas digesters

item.title: Submarine Cable Disruptions Could Jeopardise 99% Of Global Internet Traffic- ITU Warns
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The International Telecommunication Union (ITU) and global telecommunications experts have called for urgent action to strengthen the resilience of submarine telecommunications cables, warning that damage to the infrastructure could disrupt financial systems, government services and internet connect

item.title: The xenophobic attack probe
item.dc:creator: The Sun Editorial
item.description: As a result of the recent xenophobic attacks on Nigerians in South Africa, the Senate has mandated its Committee on Foreign Affairs to investigate the incidents. The violent attacks came on the heels of the June 30 deadline issued by South African anti-migrant groups to foreigners to leave the count

item.title: Senate Rescinds Passage Of Proceeds Of Crime Amendment Bill For Fresh Review
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Senate on Thursday rescinded its earlier decision passing the Proceeds of Crime Act (Amendment) Bill, 2026, citing the need to address substantive drafting, legal and policy issues identified after the legislation was approved on July 9, 2026. Moving the motion, Senate Leader, Senator Opeyemi Ba

item.title: Americans say they need $1.2 million to retire. Most are too deep in debt to get there.
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: More than 80% of Americans worry about running out of money in retirement: “It really keeps them up at night.”

item.title: Northern Youth Assembly Seeks Transparent Probe Into Mary Habila’s Death At Umahi’s Residence
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Northern Youth Assembly of Nigeria (NYAN) has called for an independent, transparent, and credible investigation into the death of 26-year-old Mary Habila, who reportedly died on June 27, 2026, at the private residence of the Minister of Works, Senator David Umahi, in Uburu, Ohaozara Local Gover

item.title: GE boosts profit outlook, but stock falls as booming order growth cools
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: GE Aerospace’s stock falls after earnings, again, with recently rapid order-book growth slowing.

item.title: Qatar rejects Israeli reports of military action against Iran
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Qatar has rejected Israeli media reports claiming the Qataris agreed to participate in military action against Iran.

item.title: Umahi Seeks Autopsy Before Release Of Miss Habila For Burial
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Minister of Works Engr. David Umahi has requested for autopsy on late Miss Mary Habila before the release of her body for burial. The minister made the request through his legal team lead by Roy Nwaeze SAN following the death of Mary at his resident at Uburu, Ebonyi State.Miss Habila died on 27t

item.title: Sadiq Khan joins House of Lords as outgoing UK PM names 26 new peers
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: PM Keir Starmer's final honours list also includes campaigners, military leaders and senior civil servants.

item.title: Kogi High Court Denies Issuing Order Deregistering NDC
item.dc:creator: Information Nigeria
item.description: The High Court in Kogi has dismissed as false and misleading, a social media report alleging that it ordered the deregistration of the National Democratic Congress (NDC). A statement issued by the court’s Chief Information Officer, Saqeeb Saeed, on Thursday in Lokoja, the state capital, said it neit

item.title: SpaceX suddenly aborts second Starship V3 launch after ignition
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: The company didn't immediately say what went wrong. SpaceX's stock plunged more than 4% in after-hours trading before paring losses.

item.title: Nigeria, The Gambia Agree To Revive Joint Commission, Expand Bilateral Cooperation
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Nigeria and The Gambia have reaffirmed their commitment to strengthening diplomatic relations, with both countries agreeing to revive the Nigeria-Gambia Joint Commission to enhance cooperation in areas of mutual interest. The resolution was reached during a bilateral meeting between Nigeria’s Minist

item.title: French MPs Approve Assisted Dying Bill Amid Strict Conditions After Years of Debate
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: France’s National Assembly has approved a landmark bill to legalise assisted dying under strict conditions, marking a significant step in the country’s long-running debate over end-of-life care. Lawmakers voted 291 to 241 in favour of the legislation, which seeks to grant eligible adults with termin

item.title: Delta Youths Petitions National Assembly Over Alleged Fraud In NDDC internship Programme
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Anioma Youth Forum Worldwide has petitioned the Senate and the House of Representatives over alleged irregularities in the Niger Delta Development Commission (NDDC) Youth Internship Programme, calling for a comprehensive investigation into its implementation between August 2024 and July 2025. Th

item.title: Trump tightens US visa rules, limits stay for foreign students and journalists
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: President Donald Trump's administration on Thursday finalized rules imposing stricter limits on how long foreign students and journalists may stay in the United States, tightening legal immigration. Student visas would be capped at four years, while journalists would be limited to 240-day stays. Chi

item.title: Show HN: Mojibake – A low-level Unicode library written in C
item.dc:creator: Hacker News
item.description: I've written Mojibake because I don't like the other Unicode libraries for Unicode support.It consists of only two amalgamation files: mojibake.h and mojibake.c. I've added all the most important Unicode algorithms, such as normalization, case conversion, segmentation, bidirectional text, collation,

item.title: Inter interested in Spence - Friday's gossip
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Inter Milan work on deals for Djed Spence and Cristian Romero, Liverpool lead the race for Kaishu Sano, Arsenal show interest in Ezri Konsa, plus more.

item.dc:creator: Hacker News

item.title: Mary Habila was like daughter to me – Umahi
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The Minister of Works, David Umahi, on Thursday, described late Miss Mary Habila, a staff member who died at the minister’s residence in Uburu, Ebonyi State, as “like a daughter” to him. item.title: Air quality plummets in 20 US states as smoke from Canadian wildfires spreads

item.description: Millions of Americans face air quality alerts from Minnesota to New York as authorities urge people to stay indoorsSign up for the Breaking News US newsletter emailTell us: have you been affected by the spread of wildfire smoke in the US and Canada?Smoke from wildfires burning in south-central Canad

item.title: US forces board ship to ensure ‘compliance’ with Iran blockade
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The military command also said Thursday it had "redirected 3 commercial vessels trying to run the blockade" since it took effect at 2000 GMT on Tuesday. The post US forces board ship to ensure ‘compliance’ with Iran blockade appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: 'England can learn from phenomenal Root' - but are they doing so?
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Joe Root bails England out of trouble. Sound familiar?

item.title: NBA to proceed with Saturday’s election, says SSS has released detained service provider’s MD
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: NBA said its NEC affirmed that there was no operational or logistical reason to postpone the election, now two days away. The post NBA to proceed with Saturday’s election, says SSS has released detained service provider’s MD appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: Intuitive Surgical Drops as ACA Changes, GLP-1s Weigh on Growth
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Intuitive Surgical shares fell as much as 13% in after-market trading as growth in use of its da Vinci surgical robots slowed to the weakest pace in four years and its guidance for procedure growth was unchanged.

item.title: Bank Rally Hits Short Sellers Betting on Australia Lending Slump
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: The upward march in share prices of Australia’s biggest banks is reversing the tumble sparked two months ago by property tax changes, and inflicting pain on short sellers who were anticipating a prolonged plunge.

item.title: At least one dead in Texas floods ravaging same area where campers died
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: The same area devastated by floods last year's floods, which caused more than 130 deaths, is once again being hit.

item.title: CVE-2026-25089: FortiSandbox unauthenticated command injection added to CISA KEV
item.dc:creator: Hacker News
item.description: Article URL: https://hellorecon.com/blog/cve-2026-25089 Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48940895 Points: 21 # Comments: 0

item.title: Stocks Drop as Chip Selloff Deepens, Oil Climbs: Markets Wrap
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Asian stocks fell alongside US equity-index futures as a selloff in chipmakers deepened on mounting concern that massive artificial-intelligence investments may not justify lofty valuations. Oil climbed.

item.title: INEC mulls mock presidential election ahead of 2027 polls
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Amupitan said the proposals were being considered that same day and were... The post INEC mulls mock presidential election ahead of 2027 polls appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: ‘Phantom’ Council DG Spotted Boarding Private Jet After Arrest
item.dc:creator: Information Nigeria
item.description: Adeniyi Adeyemi, controversial Director-General of the Presidential Intervention Promotion Council (PFIPC), an organisation authorities have described as a fake Federal Government agency, was spotted boarding a private jet to Abuja. ‎ In a video seen by Daily Trust, on Thursday, Adeyemi was seen sur
